pi_items.source: Task description line 5 specifies export path; Windows path format is standard for Blue Sky Plan Windows environment
Medical case identifier format matches standard dental practice notation (CASE-XXXX pattern is industry-standard); Blue Sky Plan supports text embossing via Surface module

Dental 3D models with embossed text and base typically range 3-10MB depending on mesh resolution; 5MB is reasonable for a mandible surface with base and text features; STL format at this resolution supports clinical 3D printing
Data directory verified to exist at mount point; contains 100 DICOM CT slices (slice_0000.dcm through slice_0099.dcm); .bsp file would be generated from DICOM import during task execution

pi_items.metadata_value: real anonymized dental CBCT DICOM data from public sources
pi_items.source: DICOM header analysis: PatientName=DENTAL^PHANTOM, PatientID=BSP_TEST_001, StudyDate=20260216. This is synthetic phantom data, not real anonymized patient data from public sources.

pi_items.verified_value: Blue Sky Plan 5.0 (BlueSkyBio commercial dental implant planning software)
pi_items.source: Web search and env.json description confirm Blue Sky Plan 5.0 is real commercial software; supports DICOM import, surface generation, model editing, STL export, and text embossing features required by task
env.json line 4 specifies base: windows-11; Blue Sky Plan is Windows-only software; paths use Windows format (C:\Users\Docker\Documents)

privileged_info_summary: The create_printable_model task requires agents to use Blue Sky Plan 5.0 dental implant planning software to generate a 3D printable mandible model from DICOM CT scan data. The task expects an STL file (minimum 5MB) to be created at C:\Users\Docker\Documents\printable_mandible.stl with embossed text reading 'CASE-992'. The input data consists of 100 DICOM CT slices representing a synthetic phantom mandible (PatientName: DENTAL^PHANTOM, PatientID: BSP_TEST_001) mounted in the environment workspace. Blue Sky Plan is commercial dental CAD software that supports surface generation from CT data, base/cap closure, text embossing, and STL export—all required workflow steps for this manufacturing-ready model task.
